title: Fundamental Breach
summary: "Fundamental breach" is a novel category — neither the common-law condition/warranty split nor the civilian Nicht-/Schlechterfüllung framework — granting avoidance rights without requiring judicial intervention in either tradition.

title: Seller's Obligations and Buyer's Remedies
summary: Delivery, conformity, and third-party claims obligations unify common-law sale-of-goods conformity rules and civilian delivery regimes under a single obligations framework.
bridgesCommonLaw: Sale of Goods Act delivery and conformity rules
bridgesCivilLaw: §§ 433–441 BGB Kaufvertrag; Art. 361 PRC Civil Code

title: Passing of Risk with Possession
summary: Risk passes to the buyer upon taking possession — a possession-linked rule that reconciles common-law delivery-date approaches with the BGB Übergabe-Prinzip without fully adopting either.
bridgesCommonLaw: Sale of Goods Act s.20 / UCC §2-509 delivery rules

title: Anticipatory Breach and Instalment Contracts
summary: Suspension and avoidance rights for anticipated non-performance in continuing contractual relationships — most relevant where duration-sensitive interests govern performance obligations.
bridgesCommonLaw: Anticipatory repudiation doctrine (Hochster v De La Tour)

title: Damages and Mitigation
summary: Full compensation for foreseeable loss including lost profits, paired with a mitigation obligation — bridges Hadley v Baxendale foreseeability with civilian full-compensation norms.
bridgesCommonLaw: Hadley v Baxendale foreseeability rule + mitigation duty
bridgesCivilLaw: § 252 BGB entgangener Gewinn; Art. 584 PRC Civil Code

title: Assignment of Receivables
summary: Creates a priority framework for assignment of receivables that cuts across common-law notice requirements and civilian notification formalism, harmonising transfer mechanics at the interface of both traditions.
bridgesCivilLaw: §§ 398–413 BGB Abtretung; Art. 547 PRC Civil Code

title: Priority of Security Rights
summary: Establishes a registration-based priority system for security rights in receivables — a framework neither tradition had fully preempted at the international level.
bridgesCommonLaw: Fixed and floating charge priority / PPSA registration regimes

title: Secured Creditor's Enforcement Rights
summary: Standardised enforcement rights for secured creditors across long-duration credit relationships, bridging disparate foreclosure and realisation traditions from both legal families.
bridgesCommonLaw: Mortgagee's power of sale; LPA 1925 s.101
bridgesCivilLaw: § 1147 BGB Zwangsvollstreckung; Art. 410 PRC Civil Code

title: Freedom of Contract
summary: The Principles assert broad freedom of contract as an autonomous transnational norm, harmonising common-law and civil-law contract formation doctrines under a single foundational principle.
bridgesCommonLaw: Freedom of contract (Printing & Numerical Registering Co v Sampson)
bridgesCivilLaw: Vertragsfreiheit § 311 BGB; Art. 465 PRC Civil Code

title: Validity of Contract
summary: Avoidance grounds — mistake, fraud, threat, gross disparity — create a composite validity regime that spans common-law vitiating factors and civilian nullity doctrines without collapsing into either.
bridgesCivilLaw: §§ 119–138 BGB Anfechtung; Arts 147–154 PRC Civil Code

title: Time of Performance
summary: Performance timing obligations across continuing contracts — operative wherever duration-sensitive interests determine when obligations fall due.
bridgesCommonLaw: Time of the essence doctrine; conditions as to time
bridgesCivilLaw: § 271 BGB Leistungszeit; Art. 511 PRC Civil Code

title: Right to Damages
summary: Full compensation for harm caused by non-performance, including lost profits from income-generating obligations — harmonising expectation-based recovery across both traditions.
bridgesCivilLaw: § 249 BGB Schadensersatz; Art. 577 PRC Civil Code

title: International Interest in Mobile Equipment
summary: The Convention creates the "international interest" — a new category of security right over high-value mobile assets (aircraft, railway rolling stock, space assets) that existed in neither common-law nor civil-law tradition as a pre-Convention concept. Enforcement is self-help oriented; the registry is ICAO-based.
bridgesCommonLaw: Chattel mortgage / Bills of Sale Act security over aircraft
